CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-26-2016 Continued To Nov-09-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Fraud And Oppression Allegations Requesting Punitive Damages From Plaintiff'S First Amended Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorties; And [Proposed] Order
Off Calendar Aug-04-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT WENJIE SU'S Motion To Strike Fraud And Oppression Allegations Requesting Punitive Damages From Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint is ordered OFF CALENDAR; the parties entered a July 21, 2016 stipulation that resolves the motion.

Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Laura Martinez, CSR #11332, Bus. Tel: 415-551-3745. (D302)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-27-2016 Continued To Sep-14-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion To Strike Punitive Damages Allegations From Complaint
Off Calendar Jun-07-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT WENJIE SU'S Motion To Strike Punitive Damages Allegations From Complaint is granted with twenty days leave to amend. To properly allege punitive damages in a motor vehicle accident action, a plaintiff needs to "establish that the defendant was aware of the probable dangerous consequences of his conduct, and that he wilfully and deliberately failed to avoid those consequences." (Taylor v. Superior Court (1979) 24 Cal.3d 890, 896.) The allegation that a driver drove while intoxicated, by itself, is not always enough to support a claim for punitive damages. (See Taylor v. Superior Court (1979) 24 Cal.3d 890, 892 ; Dawes v. Superior Court (1980) 111 Cal.App.3d 82, 89.) Here, Plaintiff is provided leave to amend the complaint to allege facts, if he can do so in good faith, that Defendant drove while aware of the probable dangerous consequences of his conduct, that after the accident Defendant made fraudulent representations to the police about who was driving the vehicle, and that describe the circumstances of the accident and Defendant's driving. Order signed.

Judge: Suzanne Bolanos. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
